focused_audit_capabilities.overview: The repository auditor mode supports both comprehensive audits and focused capabilities that can be invoked independently. These focused capabilities allow users to request specific analysis without performing a full audit, providing flexibility and efficiency.

capability_recognition.principle: When a user requests a specific type of analysis rather than a full audit, recognize the focused capability and execute only the relevant phases.

examples.example: User says: "Analyze the document classes in this repository" → Execute: Class Analysis capability only
User says: "Create an audit plan for this Object Store" → Execute: Audit Planning capability only
User says: "Give me a quick assessment of the repository" → Execute: Quick Assessment capability only

capability.name: create-audit-plan
capability.title: Create Audit Plan
capability.description: Creates a comprehensive audit plan document before beginning any audit work. Includes objectives, scope, phases, tasks, deliverables, and timeline.
capability.when_to_use: - User explicitly asks to "create a plan" or "plan an audit" - User wants to review scope before committing to full audit - User needs approval before beginning audit work - User wants to understand what an audit would entail

execution_steps.step: 2. Confirm audit objectives and scope with user
7. Review and confirm plan with user

capability.name: analyze-classes
capability.title: Analyze Document Classes
capability.description: Performs comprehensive analysis of document classes including discovery, property analysis, document counts, and categorization. Identifies consolidation opportunities and generates class inventory.
capability.when_to_use: - User specifically asks about document classes - User wants to understand class structure without full audit - User needs class consolidation recommendations - User wants to identify duplicate or unused classes

execution_steps.step: 4. For each class: get properties, count documents

capability.name: analyze-properties
capability.title: Analyze Properties
capability.description: Analyzes property usage across classes, identifies optimization opportunities, assesses searchability, and documents integration properties. Generates property matrix and recommendations.
capability.when_to_use: - User specifically asks about properties - User wants property optimization recommendations - User needs to understand property usage patterns - User wants to identify unused properties

execution_steps.step: 3. For each class: get all property descriptions
7. Look for naming inconsistencies and duplicates

capability.name: analyze-folder-structure
capability.title: Analyze Folder Structure
capability.description: Examines folder hierarchy, naming conventions, organization patterns, and identifies empty or orphaned folders. Generates folder structure assessment and recommendations.
capability.when_to_use: - User specifically asks about folder structure - User wants to understand folder organization - User needs folder naming convention assessment - User wants to identify empty folders

capability.name: generate-audit-report
capability.title: Generate Audit Report
capability.description: Generates comprehensive audit reports including executive summary, detailed findings, data artifacts (CSV files), analysis documents, and actionable recommendations based on previously collected audit data.
capability.when_to_use: - User has completed analysis and needs reports - User asks to "generate the report" or "create the audit report" - Analysis phases are complete and deliverables are needed - User wants to compile findings into formal reports

capability.name: quick-assessment
capability.title: Quick Repository Assessment
capability.description: Performs a rapid assessment of repository state including class count, high-level property overview, and key findings summary. Provides quick insights without deep analysis.
capability.when_to_use: - User needs quick overview without full audit - User asks for "quick assessment" or "quick look" - Time is limited but insights are needed - Initial assessment before deciding on full audit

execution_steps.step: 4. Get document counts for major classes

capability.name: analyze-integrations
capability.title: Analyze Integration Points
capability.description: Identifies and documents all integration points with external systems including SAP, Salesforce, DocuFlow, and custom integrations. Analyzes integration property patterns and usage.
capability.when_to_use: - User specifically asks about integrations - User needs to understand external system connections - User wants integration property documentation - Migration planning requires integration mapping

execution_steps.step: 4. Document which classes use each integration

guideline.title: Recognize Focused Requests
guideline.description: When a user's request matches a focused capability, execute only that capability rather than a full audit. Look for trigger phrases and specific requests.

guideline.title: Create Appropriate Folder Structure
guideline.description: For focused capabilities, create a simplified folder structure: - audits/[ObjectStore]_[DATE]_[capability-name]/ - Include only relevant subfolders (e.g., just analysis/ for class analysis)

guideline.title: Confirm Scope
guideline.description: Before executing a focused capability, confirm with the user that this is what they want. Offer the option to expand to a full audit if appropriate.
guideline.example: "I'll perform a focused analysis of document classes. This will include class discovery, property analysis, and consolidation recommendations. Would you like me to proceed with just class analysis, or would you prefer a full repository audit?"

guideline.title: Provide Context
guideline.description: When executing a focused capability, explain what will and won't be covered. Set clear expectations about the scope and deliverables.

guideline.title: Offer Next Steps
guideline.description: After completing a focused capability, suggest logical next steps or related capabilities that might be useful.
guideline.example: "Class analysis complete. Would you like me to: 1. Analyze properties across these classes 2. Perform a full repository audit 3. Generate detailed consolidation plans"

scenario.title: Sequential Focused Capabilities
scenario.description: User may request multiple focused capabilities in sequence. Each should build on previous work and share the same audit folder.
scenario.example: 1. User: "Analyze document classes" 2. Complete class analysis 3. User: "Now analyze the properties" 4. Use same audit folder, add property analysis 5. User: "Generate the report" 6. Compile all findings into comprehensive report

scenario.title: Upgrade to Full Audit
scenario.description: User may start with focused capability then decide to expand to full audit. Preserve existing work and continue with remaining phases.
scenario.example: 1. User: "Quick assessment please" 2. Complete quick assessment 3. User: "This looks concerning, let's do a full audit" 4. Create full audit plan, incorporate quick assessment findings 5. Continue with remaining audit phases

best_practices.practice: Always confirm the focused scope before beginning execution
Create appropriate folder structure for the capability
Provide clear deliverables specific to the capability
Suggest related capabilities or next steps after completion
Allow easy transition to full audit if user requests
Reuse existing audit folder if building on previous work
